passport_photo.name: Passport Size Photo
steps: Visit any nearby photo studio or browsing centre
Ask for 'passport size' colour photos on white background
Get 6–10 copies printed (₹30–60 total)
Keep digital copy on your phone as backup

bank_account.name: Bank Account
bank_account.where_to_get: Any Nationalised Bank, Post Office, or Jan Dhan branch
steps: Visit nearest bank branch or post office
Request to open a 'Basic Savings Bank Deposit Account' (BSBD / Jan Dhan) – free of cost
Submit Aadhaar Card as KYC proof
Submit one passport-size photo
Collect your passbook on the same day or within 3 days

disability_certificate.name: Disability Certificate
disability_certificate.where_to_get: Government Civil Hospital / District Medical Board
steps: Visit the nearest government civil hospital
Request a referral to the District Medical Board (DMB)
Undergo medical examination by a panel of doctors at the DMB
Submit Aadhaar card, residence proof, and 2 passport-size photos
Collect the certificate after 7–15 working days (may be issued same-day at some hospitals)

aadhaar_card.name: Aadhaar Card
aadhaar_card.where_to_get: Aadhaar Enrolment Centre (Common Service Centre / Post Office / Bank)
steps: Locate nearest Aadhaar centre via uidai.gov.in or mAadhaar app
Carry original documents: birth certificate / passport / ration card for age proof, and any address proof
Biometric capture (fingerprints + iris scan) done at centre
Collect acknowledgement slip with Enrolment ID
Download e-Aadhaar from myaadhaar.uidai.gov.in in 5–7 days
aadhaar_card.cost: Free for new enrolment; ₹50 for updates
documents_to_bring: Date of Birth Proof (Birth Certificate / Passport)

income_certificate.name: Income Certificate
income_certificate.where_to_get: Tehsildar / Mamlatdar Office or online on state government portal
steps: Visit your nearest Tehsildar (Revenue) office
Fill Form for Income Certificate (available free at office)
Attached required documents (Aadhaar, self-declaration of income, bank statement or salary slip)
Submit form at the counter and take acknowledgement slip
Collect certificate in 3–7 working days or download from state portal
documents_to_bring: Salary slip or agricultural income proof

residence_proof.name: Residence Proof
residence_proof.where_to_get: Any document that proves where you currently live
steps: Collect any ONE of: Ration Card, Voter ID, Utility Bill (electricity/water/gas), Aadhaar Card (if address is updated), Bank passbook with address
Ensure the document shows your CURRENT residential address
If address is not updated on Aadhaar, get Aadhaar address update or use another document
Self-attested photocopy is accepted in most applications
residence_proof.estimated_days: Immediate if document is already available

bonafide_certificate.name: Bonafide Certificate
steps: Visit the administrative office of your school or college
Request a Bonafide Certificate from the Principal or Dean
Fill in the prescribed application form (if any)
Collect the certificate within 1–3 working days
Ensure it has the institution seal and authorised signature

student_id.name: Student ID Card
student_id.where_to_get: Your School / College / University Administrative Office
steps: Visit your institution's administrative office
Request student ID card if not already issued
Submit passport-size photo and enrolment number
Collect ID card within 1–5 working days

previous_marksheet.name: Previous Year Marksheet
previous_marksheet.where_to_get: Your School / College or Examination Board
steps: Collect your original marksheet from school/college records
If lost, apply for a duplicate at the examination board office
Bring admit card number and roll number for duplicate request
Download digital marksheet from DigiLocker (digilocker.gov.in) for many boards
previous_marksheet.estimated_days: Immediate if original available; 7–15 days for duplicate
documents_to_bring: Previous admit card or roll number

resume.name: Resume / CV
steps: Create a resume with your name, age, educational qualifications, skills, and work experience
Include contact details and Aadhaar number
Get it typed at any internet cafe (₹15–30) if needed
Use free tools: Canva, Google Docs, or Microsoft Word templates
Get 4–6 photocopies made for multiple applications

educational_certificates.name: Educational Certificates
educational_certificates.where_to_get: Your Previous Schools / Colleges or Examination Board
steps: Gather mark sheets and degree/diploma certificates from all previous educational institutions
Download digital copies from DigiLocker (digilocker.gov.in) – free
For missing or lost originals, contact the examination board for certified duplicates
Get self-attested photocopies made for each application
educational_certificates.estimated_days: Immediate from DigiLocker; 7–30 days for physical certificates

employment_exchange_reg.name: Employment Exchange Registration
steps: Visit the nearest Employment Exchange office (find via your state government website)
Fill registration form (Form A) available at the counter
Submit Aadhaar, educational certificates, and passport-size photo
Collect registration card on the same day or in 1–2 days
Renew registration annually to keep it active

health_card.name: Health Card / Ayushman Card
health_card.where_to_get: Common Service Centre (CSC) or nearest Empanelled Hospital
steps: Check eligibility on pmjay.gov.in or the MERA PMJAY app
Visit nearest Common Service Centre (CSC) or empanelled hospital
Bring Aadhaar Card and ration card
Get biometric verification done
Ayushman card downloaded or issued on spot

medical_reports.name: Medical Reports / Prescriptions
steps: Visit the doctor who is currently treating your disability
Request a summary of medical diagnosis, ongoing treatment, and medication
At government hospital: ask for OPD prescription and any blood test / imaging reports
Keep originals and make 4–5 photocopies for different applications

bpl_card.name: BPL Card (Below Poverty Line Ration Card)
bpl_card.where_to_get: Gram Panchayat Office or District Supply Office
steps: Contact the Gram Panchayat or nearest Ration / Supply office
Fill the BPL Survey form and submit with Aadhaar and income certificate
Village officer / social worker may visit for verification
BPL card issued within 15–30 days after verification
Alternative: obtain income certificate showing annual income < ₹1 lakh as proxy
